title: Matched-pair prompt record
description: Schema for a single prompt record in the tool-calibration matched-pair corpus. Each record must be addressable by stable id, sliceable on every analysis axis, and explicitly linked to its pair.

id.description: Globally unique prompt id, format `{tool}_{domain}_{difficulty}_{disambiguator}_{NNN}_{shortuuid}`. See id_scheme.py.

pair_id.description: Pair identifier shared by both halves of a matched pair. Identical to id minus the trailing _shortuuid.

condition.description: Which half of the matched comparison this record represents.

pair_type.description: A = difficulty manipulation (task changes, affordance constant). B = affordance manipulation (task constant, available tools differ).

tool_target.description: Which tool this prompt is designed to probe. `none` for control prompts that should not warrant any tool.

domain.description: Coarse topical bucket. Free-form for now; see open questions in investigation.md.

sub_domain.description: Optional finer-grained slice within `domain`. Free-form. Use when `domain` alone loses important structure.

difficulty_label.description: Human-assigned difficulty for the prompt. The intent: at `trivial`/`easy` a target model reliably succeeds; at `hard`/`extreme` it reliably fails. `medium` is the calibration boundary.

difficulty_calibrated.description: Empirical difficulty per target model, keyed by model id. Populated post-calibration.

frequency_class.description: Prior on prompt-shape frequency in real LLM use. Used to stratify analysis (do agents calibrate worse on edge cases?).

register.description: Dominant register of the user_prompt. Pick the most-dominant aspect when multiple apply; capture nuance in register_notes.

register_notes.description: Optional free-form nuance about register that the enum can't capture.

system_prompt_id.description: Pointer to a system prompt template in system_prompts/manifest.json.

token_count.description: Token count of user_prompt under the target tokenizer. Optional; populated during corpus prep.

expected_tool_call.description: Whether a well-calibrated agent should call any tool on this prompt under the linked system_prompt_id.

expected_call_confidence.description: How confident the curator is in expected_tool_call. Low = the prompt sits at the calibration boundary deliberately.

expected_pair_behavior.description: One-line description of how this record should differ from its pair_id sibling. Used as a sanity check when reviewing pairs.

calibration_status.description: `assumed` until an empirical calibration run validates the difficulty. `verified` after at least one target-model run agrees. `contested` if calibration disagrees with the human label.

calibration_verified_on.description: Date the calibration was verified, if applicable.

source.description: Where this prompt came from. hand_curated = a human wrote it; llm_generated = produced in bulk generation (A3); transformed = derived from another record.

source_record_id.description: If source is `transformed`, the id of the parent record.
